claim_boundary.next_gate: report the failed snapshot and condition without changing seeds, snapshots, or thresholds
claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: current calibrated backend evidence, provider access, hardware execution, independent transcript evidence, protocol or cryptographic soundness, sampling hardness, quantum advantage, BQP separation, or B10 credit
claim_boundary.what_is_supported: A preregistered local Aer replay driven by frozen historical IBM QPU snapshot properties.

requirements.label: contract matches the publicly posted SHA-256
contract binds the accepted R124 payload
Qiskit IBM Runtime version matches the software contract
all frozen snapshot hashes match the preregistration
R125 root seeds are disjoint from R123 and R124
all compiled circuits preserve logical classical-bit order
all trial rows are transpiled and preserve paired evidence
all holdout schedules have unique hashes
block, leave-one-out, and transition ledgers are complete
all five preregistered conditions are evaluated per snapshot
historical snapshots are not mislabeled as current or hardware evidence
no soundness, advantage, BQP, or new-credit claim is promoted

minimum leave-one-block-out Wilson lower reaches the floor
every block reaches the point floor
paired pass-to-fail transitions stay within the ceiling
